data.title: Mommy Scrubs wins $35,000 in eosera® Foundation Pitch Competition
data.description:
Mommy Scrubs wins $35,000 in eosera® Foundation Pitch Competition

Mommy Scrubs wins $35,000 in eosera® Foundation Pitch Competition

PR Newswire

FORT WORTH, Texas, Nov. 15, 2025

FORT WORTH, Texas, Nov. 15, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- A McKinney woman who designed a postpartum scrub line for breastfeeding mothers in healthcare took away $35,000 during the fourth annual eosera® Foundation Pitch Competition Thursday night at the Kimbell Museum.

Cherie Turner, CEO and Founder of Mommy Scrubs, could hardly hold back the tears as her name was announced by event emcee, NBC 5 anchor, Deborah Ferguson. She won the $5,000 people's choice award, as well as the $30,000 first place award.

"I am just overjoyed and overwhelmed with gratitude," Turner said. "This is just a perfect, right-on-time blessing for Mommy Scrubs."

The competition, hosted by the eosera® Foundation, invites women founders with businesses fewer than three years old to apply. This year, three finalists were selected from more than 250 applicants nationwide. This is the first year the competition was opened to women outside the state of Texas, where eosera® is based.

Fort Worth Mayor Mattie Parker kicked off the event, welcoming guests to the competition and citing some important statistics. She mentioned a recent Wells Fargo report which states Dallas-Fort Worth is now No. 2 in the nation for growth of women-owned businesses.

"So we are on the right track here in Fort Worth," she said.

Parker said Fort Worth is now the tenth largest city in the country, which calls for it to act like a world class city.

"You cannot be a world class city without pouring into the next generation of leaders, and it does start with the bedrock of the US economy, which is small businesses and entrepreneurs," Parker said, before encouraging everyone in the audience to cheer loudly for the finalists, as it takes "tremendous guts and bravery to build a business from scratch."

CEO of eosera®, Elyse Stoltz Dickerson, knows this first-hand. She won a pitch competition a decade ago when she was starting her now multi-million-dollar ear care business. This experience inspired her to give back to other women founders by providing them with valuable seed funding through the eosera® Foundation.

"Each year the pitch competition just tops the year before in energy and excitement," Stoltz Dickerson said. "I always walk away feeling so inspired by the finalists and energized by the audience who came to support them. It's truly my favorite night of the year."

Stoltz Dickerson said she feels Turner — and Mommy Scrubs — has what it takes to be a successful business.

Turner first dreamed up Mommy Scrubs after her maternity leave ended and she returned to work as an occupational therapist in 2018. She discovered that pumping breastmilk at work often meant fully undressing, her back against a cold chair. It was inconvenient and uncomfortable, but it sparked an idea — scrubs designed specifically for breastfeeding and pumping healthcare moms.

Turner got to work testing and designing new scrubs that snap down in the front for easy pumping access, while maintaining the standard function of scrubs in a healthcare setting. Her patent-pending design has been rolled out to a smaller audience, but now it's ready for the next step — paid marketing.

"We will be able to put Mommy Scrubs in front of the faces who need it, so we're really excited to start our paid marketing efforts and more people will know about Mommy Scrubs," she said.

In second place was Annika Lundstrom, CEO of ReMinded, a company that is commercializing the first clinical-grade, rapid saliva test that tracks cortisol, the body's stress hormone. Lundstrom, whose company is based in New York City, won $5,000.

"It was a much larger event than I expected," Lundstrom said. "And it's just great to see this type of traction that the [women entrepreneur] community is gaining now. I felt that this event just gave us that extra push of powering through the last months of 2025 and going into a strong 2026."

In third place, winning $2,500 — was Melissa Wood, CEO of Formus, an AI tool for home design professionals that saves time and money.

Wood said it was an honor to be a part of an organization that supports women founders.

"As they say, 2% of women get venture capital funding, so opportunities like this mean the world, and I just appreciate having the stage to share my company," she said.

This year's competition was sponsored by iHeartImpact, Simmons Bank, Higginbotham, and Satori Capitol — along with many other Fort Worth businesses who pitched in for a drawing valued at over $2,000.

"We are so proud to be a part of this event supporting women's entrepreneurship, and we're looking forward to being a part of it in future years," said Brad Geminder, Senior Account Direct at iHeartMedia.

"I'm in awe of these finalists, taking their businesses from idea to execution," said Tamara Wren, iHeartMedia Account Development Manager.

 Lori Baldock, Forth Worth Market President for Simmons Bank, said the bank has sponsored the competition since its first year.

"We see this not as an expense, but as an investment — an investment in the eosera® Foundation and these women entrepreneurs," she said. "As a female executive myself, there is nothing more rewarding than seeing our dollars spent and invested in the growth of women-owned businesses."

For more information about the eosera® Foundation Pitch Competition, contact kimberly.ikpo@eosera.com.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/mommy-scrubs-wins-35-000-in-eosera-foundation-pitch-competition-302616275.html

SOURCE Eosera

data.title: What's our FIRE number? We make $475,000 together and have more than $2 million saved
data.description:
By Alessandra Malito

'I think If I live in India between the ages of 60 to 65 years old, I will save a lot of money.'

"We started saving $8,000 a month in the taxable account this year, and we're both maximizing our 401(k) plans." (Photo subject is a model.)

Dear Help Me Retire,

I'm 51 years old and my wife is 46. My taxable investment account: $1.29 million in a total stock index fund. My Roth IRA: $19,000 in a total stock index. My 401(k): $830,000 in an S&P 500 fund. My wife's 401(k): $194,000 in an S&P 500 fund. I do not have an HSA.

My salary is $325,000, and my wife's salary is $150,000. We have one kid in college and have a 529 plan with $195,000 - enough for four years. Our vehicles are paid off. For debt, we have a 3.25% mortgage with a $250,000 balance on a $730,000 home, paying $2,300 per month.

My Social Security benefit will be $5,025 at 70 years old, according to the Social Security Administration. My wife's benefit would be $1,980 at 67 years old. We're trying to cut down monthly expenses to $11,000 from $12,000.

We started saving $8,000 a month in the taxable account this year, and we're both maximizing our 401(k) plans. I will be starting a backdoor Roth for my wife this year and continue to contribute mine.

My questions are:

1. What should be my FIRE number? Is it $12,000 multiplied by 12 multiplied by 25, or $3.6 million?

2. When should I do a Roth conversion? When I am between 60 and 70 years old?

3. I plan to retire at 60. I think if I live in India between the ages of 60 to 65 years old, I will save a lot of money due to the cheaper health insurance, and can easily maintain my lifestyle at $25,000 per year. My parents have a house over there. What do you think about my plan?

4. Should I pay a robo adviser to do tax-loss harvesting in my taxable brokerage account?

Squaring Away Retirement

Do you have questions about retirement, Social Security, where to live or how to afford it at all? We want to hear from you. Join the conversation in our Facebook community: Retire Better with MarketWatch.

Related: I want to keep my job and claim Social Security as soon as possible. How much will I get?

Dear Squaring Away,

I appreciate all of the planning and intricate details. First and foremost, great job.

Given how well your family has planned its finances, you have options. The earlier you fully retire, the longer the money you've saved has to stretch, especially if you expect to live a long life. And even more if you anticipate health issues later down the road, since healthcare can be quite expensive, regardless of your insurance coverage.

There's no one rule for calculating your FIRE ("financial independence, retire early") number. There are so many iterations, with the most common called "lean FIRE" or "fat FIRE." The former is saving the bare minimum to quit your job, while the latter is stocking up as much money as you possibly can before you leave the workforce.

Although the word "retirement" is part of FIRE, a lot of people who pursue this lifestyle are generating income through blogs, speaking arrangements, or consulting work. You should factor that into your plans too - maybe you leave your current job, but stay up to date on your skills and network so that you can always tap in for a little extra cash flow.

Your FIRE number

Regardless, one way to arrive at your FIRE number is by multiplying your annual expenses by 25, as you've already done. This calculation uses the 4% rule, according to CPA Practice Advisor, which is how much you'd withdraw in the initial year (then adjusting for inflation as the years go on).

The 4% rule assumes a 30-year retirement, and has also been highly contested in recent years. Some people say it's too aggressive of a distribution rate, and people can get away with taking out less from their savings.

With your $2.3 million portfolio, however, and $8,000 a month in taxable savings, and dual 401(k) contributions, you're actually on track to exceed $4.5 million to $5 million by age 60 - comfortably meeting your FIRE goal.

Roth conversion

Your next question starts with a similar answer - there's no hard and fast rule for what age you should start Roth conversions; it depends on your income, the amount you're converting and your tax brackets. When you convert traditional IRA money into a Roth account, you have to pay income taxes on the conversion amount. If you're in a high tax bracket right now, it is probably not the best time to start converting.

Be careful not to push yourself into a higher tax bracket, which a qualified financial planner or accountant can help you avoid. Your ideal window to do a Roth conversion is after retirement (age 60) but before RMDs (age 73) and Social Security (age 70, if you wait to get the maximum amount possible).

Robo advisers

That brings me to your fourth question. Robo advisers are a useful tool for the do-it-yourself type of investor, but when finances start to get tricky, such as multiple life changes or big moves, a real human can make all the difference. What's more, even paying 0.25%-0.35% in fees on $1.29 million is $3,000-$4,500 a year, hardly worth it if all you need is advice on tax harvesting.

For instance, you mentioned moving to India for some of your retirement years. You could find a trustworthy and competent financial planner familiar with expats - or even more specifically, expats living in India; robo-advisers won't necessarily know what to tell you or ask you about those sorts of retirement plans. If you just require tax-loss harvesting, a digital platform might be the right move, but if you want more, like investment advice, interview some qualified professionals.

Moving to India

And lastly, your third question about moving to India. It does sound like you can live a less expensive lifestyle there, but only you and your wife and the rest of your family can determine if making such a big move is the right choice. You'll definitely want to get your family's health insurance figured out before leaving the country, so that you're covered for the little things like regular checkups and dentist visits as well as unexpected events.

Keep Medicare in the back of your mind, so that when you do hit your 65th birthday, regardless of where in the world, you're covered and not hit with any penalties when you return (such as for Part B or Part D). Here's more information about Medicare and living abroad.

The U.S. and India have a tax treaty designed to prevent your income from being taxed twice, even though you will have to file U.S. income taxes as a U.S. citizen living overseas. How much tax you pay in India will depend on your residency status. You can read more here.

Your quality of life matters just as much as your finances. You've done such an incredible job of saving and investing for the future, and thinking through all the little details like a 529 plan and FIRE number. If moving to India will help you save money and you and your wife will enjoy the experience, including living at your parent's house or being away from your life in the U.S., then go for it. If it won't bring you joy, don't do it just for the sake of a lower cost of living. Instead, talk to your family about some back up plans and run the numbers for each scenario. You might be surprised at what kind of plans you all come up with.

Retirement, be it at a "traditional age" or early, is all about balance - doing your best to make your savings work for you and having a plan to spend the money wisely, but also enjoying that time after decades of working.

By submitting your story to Dow Jones & Co., the publisher of MarketWatch, you understand and agree that we may use your story, or versions of it, in all media and platforms, including via third parties.

Have a question about your own retirement savings? Email us at HelpMeRetire@marketwatch.com

-Alessandra Malito

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  11-15-25 1239ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: If you thought getting approved for Medicaid was hard, wait until you try to use it for this
data.description:
By Beth Pinsker

Strategizing in advance can really pay off when it comes to Medicaid-funded nursing-home placements

Most people put off any sort of end-of-life planning, and that leaves family members scrambling to make decisions in a crisis.

If you thought getting approved for Medicaid was a huge slog through red tape, wait until you find out what comes next: The hustling for a placement in a good nursing home. The competition can be so intense that some families now send their loved ones out of state - which requires filing again, because Medicaid enrollment is not national, and each state has its own rules and application procedures.

Pam Krueger, an investor advocate, helped her family with that situation when her brother-in-law was ill. "We had to go in search of a facility that had a Medicaid bed, because he ran through his savings and lived beyond it. We wanted to find facilities that had the care that was needed and the quality," said Krueger, whose firm, Wealthramp, helps connect people with fiduciary financial planners.

She started to make calls and sent her niece out to scout facilities, but they had no luck near Boca Raton, Fla., where she and most of her family lived. "We finally found what he needed in St. Louis," she said. "He didn't want to move there, but that was the best possible care. Now he's super happy there."

Most people put off any sort of end-of-life planning, and that leaves the family members who want to help them scrambling to make decisions in a crisis. I was in that mode when I took care of my mother, and it made me realize that the 70% of adults in the U.S. who have no estate plan to speak of needed something of a wake-up call to get them to see how important it is to get your ducks in a row. My book, "My Mother's Money: A Guide to Financial Caregiving," dives deep into the practical things you need to know. This is an adapted excerpt.

Strategizing in advance can really pay off when it comes to Medicaid-funded nursing-home placements. If you start out with a loved one at an assisted-living facility with multiple care levels or a continuing-care community and pay for it yourself for as long as you can, you may have better luck getting one of its Medicaid slots.

That worked for my grandmother, who transitioned from independent living to assisted living to a Medicaid bed in the nursing-care unit of her community in the 1990s. But you have to watch closely and communicate with administrators often, because the situation can change and new management might not honor verbal agreements made with previous staff members. My grandmother's facility, for instance, no longer offers nursing care and therefore doesn't take Medicaid at all.

In some states, this sort of situation has led to residents essentially getting kicked out of a facility when they can no longer pay - which is, surprisingly, not against the law as long as it's done within the state's parameters, which may require proper notice and an avenue for appeal.

It's hard to think of everything at once, though, and family situations change. My college classmate Maggie had been very happy with her mother's memory care at an assisted-living facility. When her mother's money ran out, Maggie and her husband picked up the cost, which started at $9,000 a month and grew to $12,000. But then Maggie split from her husband, and the payments were suddenly a stretch. She started to look into getting her mom onto Medicaid, but the facility didn't accept it, so she also had to look for a new one.

Maggie reached out to a for-profit referral service, and a consultant directed her to a number of places in her area that would take Medicaid after the patient paid in full for two years. That didn't necessarily mean her mom would qualify for Medicaid at that time, however, because of the five-year Medicaid look-back period. It would depend on when she spent down her assets and if the payments to the assisted-living facility were categorized as gifts from the family. My friend could have still been on the hook for the full cost for five years, or a portion of it, until her mom was properly signed up.

In addition, she soon found out that the list from the agency she contacted wasn't necessarily comprehensive but rather consisted of facilities the agency contracted with for referrals. She was able to get a better recommendation from a stranger in a bagel shop who happened to overhear her talking to a friend about her situation, and that was where her mom ended up going. "I wish I had known all this earlier," Maggie said wearily.

Still another way you need to plan ahead is to keep the patient's income and assets under the limits the whole time they are enrolled in the program. Once on Medicaid, a person's Social Security payments typically go directly to the facility, and the patient is allowed only what is known as the "personal needs allowance," which varies by state but is set federally at as low as $30 a month. If a person gets a large gift or windfall of some sort, they have to report it to Medicaid, and that could jeopardize or temporarily suspend their benefits.

Alex Murguia had to pay constant attention as he managed his parents' finances through this process. He talked about it on the podcast "Retire with Style," which he co-hosts with the retirement expert Wade Pfau. At the time, Murguia's father was 95 and had dementia, and he was in a nursing home on Medicaid. Murguia's mother was in an assisted-living facility, spending down her assets, with Murguia and his siblings covering some of the costs. When his dad's balance from his personal allowance started to go over the limit, Murguia had to use it to buy things such as shampoo and body lotion.

As his mother reached the point of needing nursing care, her facility nudged Murguia to finish the paperwork to get her on Medicaid, and he was grateful for that and for its assistance with the process.

"If you're helping your parents out of pocket because they have no money, this is where Medicaid can actually help them out in a great way. There's no stigma to it. It just is what it is," he said.

-Beth Pinsker

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  11-15-25 1239ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Steph Curry likely made $300 million with Under Armour. Can his next move match Roger Federer's post-Nike success?
data.description:
By Weston Blasi

The Golden State Warriors star could take his Curry Brand independent or to another retailer - something Federer and Tiger Woods scored success with after splitting from Nike

Steph Curry's break from Under Armour shows how far the latter's brand has fallen - and how much the NBA star is betting on himself.

Stephen Curry is betting on himself again - and this time, it's with his shoes.

The Golden State Warriors legend announced Thursday that he and Under Armour (UAA) have mutually parted ways, ending a 13-year agreement that propelled the apparel brand into the basketball zeitgeist like never before. His departure coincides with Under Armour retreating from the basketball market amid struggles for the company's stock price.

Read more: Under Armour and Steph Curry are breaking up. The move is partly about 'discipline,' CEO says.

With the split finalized, Curry will retain rights to the "Curry Brand," a subdivision within Under Armour, and he intends to take it forward independently, which could include teaming with another retail partner.

The two once seemed like the perfect match: Curry was an undersized underdog from Davidson College, and Under Armour was trying to disrupt the other big sports-apparel retailers like Nike (NKE) and Adidas (XE:ADS) (ADDYY).

An individual basketball-sneaker line can produce billions of dollars in revenue. NBA superstars like Michael Jordan, LeBron James, Kevin Durant and Curry have made nearly as much off-court revenue as on-court earnings, thanks in part to their sneaker lines - but success stories have almost always come alongside a deep-pocketed partner.

Curry's deal with Under Armour catapulted him to the top of highest earners in all of sports. His initial deal with the company in 2013 was valued at a reported $4 million a year, and subsequent Under Armour partnerships for $215 million and $75 million in stock put his total lifetime earnings with the company at around $300 million.

But moving a sneaker line from a well-known brand like Under Armour to an independent one will be tricky.

"The U.S. market has massive competition with behemoth brands like Nike and Adidas, whose marketing budgets easily eclipse anything an independent brand could produce," sneaker-content creator David Daniels, who goes by SneakerPhetish on social media, told MarketWatch. "If Curry Brand goes independent, it will be a steep hill to climb."

Daniels also noted that research and development for basketball shoes is expensive, making it even more difficult for smaller industry players to stay afloat.

"It will be very expensive to try to be an independent brand," agreed Matt Powell, a sports-retail expert at consultancy Spurwink River.

Industry insiders suggest that perhaps the better play would be for Curry to team up with another brand that is already making shoes and license his Curry Brand to them, rather than try to get into the expensive shoe-manufacturing business.

"I would hope to see him align with a brand like Adidas or even an overseas brand like Anta (HK:2020) (ANPDY), home of Kyrie Irving and Klay Thompson," Daniels said.

An athlete leaving a major brand sponsorship to go independent can be risky - but that risk sometimes comes with great rewards.

Tennis legend Roger Federer ended his 24-year partnership with Nike in 2018 after the company reportedly declined to renew his contract. He then signed a massive $300 million, 10-year deal with Uniqlo (JP:9983) (FRCOY), which likely paid him much more than his earnings at Nike, though the exact terms were not reported.

The move away from Nike meant that Federer lost the rights to his iconic "RF" logo, but it still gave him huge postretirement earnings.

Additionally, since Uniqlo wasn't selling shoes, it also allowed him the freedom to acquire an equity stake in the running-shoe company On (ONON), which later went public on the New York Stock Exchange - turning Federer's $50 million investment into $500 million, per The Boardroom.

But perhaps the best comparison to Curry is golfer Tiger Woods, who left Nike in 2024 and abandoned his "TW" logo to start Sun Day Red with TaylorMade Golf. The brand is new and sells premium products like $150 golf polos, and its success is yet to be determined.

From the archives: When LeBron James chose Nike in 2003, he gave up $28 million - it could end up making him $1 billion

In a release announcing its split with Curry, Under Armour said it wanted to focus on its "core" business instead. The move comes at an uncertain time for Under Armour, which has seen its stock fall more than 50% over the last 12 months.

"I think this decision shows Under Armour's inability to connect with teen males," retail expert Powell told MarketWatch. "Under Armour stated that the basketball business was $100 million to $120 million. Assuming that most of that was Curry, this is a tiny business."

The Curry 13, Under Armour's final Curry Brand shoe, is slated for a February 2026 release. Further colorways and associated apparel collections will be made available through October 2026.

But photos and videos of Curry wearing his Under Armour shoes during his five championship runs - and in countless battles with LeBron James - will outlast the partnership.

His Warriors teammate Draymond Green posted about the move, saying Curry "built an incredible brand, an incredible line, had people wearing Under Armours like they were cool."

Before Curry, he added, "no one was wearing Under Armour in basketball."

Read on: The NBA exec who traded superstar Luka Doncic cost the team $100 million in franchise value

-Weston Blasi

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  11-15-25 1236ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: What's the true price of Thanksgiving? Trump says groceries are getting cheaper, but many Americans are still concerned about costs.
data.description:
By Charles Passy

A new survey finds Americans expect to spend $175 on food and as much as $952 overall on this Thanksgiving, including turkey, travel and decor

The cost of turkey has been running slightly higher in recent weeks compared with last year, according to market researcher NielsenIQ. But supermarket promotions abound, experts note.

Talk about a food fight.

With Thanksgiving just a couple of weeks away, there's heated debate over what the classic holiday meal will cost this year - and questions regarding how much Americans will spend, particularly after contending with the food-price inflation of recent years.

On the one hand, President Donald Trump and administration officials have been touting that this Thanksgiving will be considerably more affordable than in previous years. They've pointed to how prominent grocery retailers, including Walmart (WMT), Target (TGT), Aldi and Schnuck Markets, say they are working to bring costs down.

"America is winning the war on high prices," the White House said in a statement.

On the other hand, reports indicate Americans are still preoccupied with inflation, and that elevated prices remain an issue for many. A new survey from financial platform Chime (CHYM) found that Americans expect to spend $175 on food and as much as $952 overall on the holiday when factoring in everything from décor and travel to buying a new outfit or fixing an appliance before meal preparation begins.

The same survey found that 63% of respondents said it's expensive to get together with family during Thanksgiving. "Which undermines the whole meaning of the holiday," said Janelle Sallenave, Chime's chief spending officer.

Nevertheless, if you look at the raw data, prices are lower - in some cases by a considerable amount - or just slightly higher for many of the food items in the Thanksgiving shopping basket. Market researcher NielsenIQ found declines in the average prices of puréed pumpkin (down 15.5%), corn (down 8.5%) and pie crust (down 3%) for the four-week period ending Nov. 1, compared with the same period in 2024.

Putting a price on turkey

As for the all-important turkey (in whole form), the price is down by 7.6%, according to NielsenIQ. Other reports have suggested prices could be higher for the bird, at least in theory, because avian flu has affected production.

Either way, consumer advocates and other experts note that many retailers will run promotions on the fowl favorite when the holiday approaches as a way to get shoppers in the door.

"We're going to see specials. It's what grocery stores do," said David Anderson, a Texas A&M University livestock economist.

Indeed, many retailers have already announced deals, including the ones touted by the Trump administration. For example, Schnuck Markets, a Midwestern chain with more than 100 locations, said its prices on frozen turkey are the lowest in at least 15 years.

'We're going to see specials. It's what grocery stores do.'David Anderson, Texas A&M

But the promotion that's perhaps gotten the most attention is Walmart's under-$40 Thanksgiving package that serves 10 people, complete with a Butterball turkey (priced at 97 cents a pound) and all the ingredients for sides. Walmart said it's the lowest price for the package since it started offering the holiday meal combo a few years ago. Trump even spoke about the Walmart deal earlier this month, noting that it's 25% cheaper than a year ago.

Comparing apples and oranges

But criticism has focused on the fact that Walmart's Thanksgiving package for 2025 doesn't, in fact, contain the exact same items as last year. "Trump's numbers are off," an Associated Press report said.

White House spokesman Kush Desai responded to this wave of critical reporting, telling MarketWatch: "Pointless media gotchas won't change the fact that addressing Joe Biden's inflation crisis has been a Day One priority for President Trump, who has already lowered prices for essentials like gas and eggs and cooled inflation to an annualized 2.5% rate - half the average inflation rate under Joe Biden."

The U.S. inflation rate, as measured by the consumer-price index, was between 2.6% and 3% when President Biden left office.

Other reports suggest Americans are feeling financially pressured as they approach the holiday season.

'The perception is out there that this is going to be a more expensive holiday.'Matt Schulz, LendingTree

A LendingTree (TREE) survey found that Thanksgiving hosts expect to spend $487 on average this year for food, drinks and décor - a 13% increase from 2024. The same survey found that 60% of respondents said inflation is affecting their plans and 33% said the costs of the holiday cause financial strain. Perhaps most telling: 18% are expressing regret about hosting Thanksgiving.

Matt Schulz, chief consumer-finance analyst at LendingTree, said that, regardless of the actual price of turkey and other items, many consumers have a certain mindset this year about Turkey Day. "The perception is out there that this is going to be a more expensive holiday," he said.

As for that perception, Michael Swanson, an agricultural economist with Wells Fargo, said it may have to do with the fact that prices continue to be elevated for food items in general, as evidenced by the most recent available federal government data that show a 2.7% annual increase in the "food at home" category's costs. But, as Swanson pointed out, some of the items driving that overall increase - such as beef, up 14.7% -are not necessarily likely to be found at the Thanksgiving table.

"Those have been the nail that sticks up," Swanson said.

Even if some consumers are feeling skittish about Thanksgiving, experts are quick to point out there are always ways to economize for the holiday.

Obviously, people can find deals such as Walmart's offering. And they can ask guests to help out by bringing a dish.

Sallenave said the Chime survey found that younger generations - millennials and Gen Z members - are particularly adept at scaling back or making other changes when planning gatherings to ensure a fun but affordable Thanksgiving.

They're focusing on "what really matters - connection over perfection," she said.

-Charles Passy

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  11-15-25 1237ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: ATYR Investors Have Opportunity to Lead aTyr Pharma, Inc. Securities Fraud Lawsuit
data.description:
ATYR Investors Have Opportunity to Lead aTyr Pharma, Inc. Securities Fraud Lawsuit

ATYR Investors Have Opportunity to Lead aTyr Pharma, Inc. Securities Fraud Lawsuit

PR Newswire

NEW YORK, Nov. 15, 2025

NEW YORK, Nov. 15, 2025 /PRNewswire/ --

Why: Rosen Law Firm, a global investor rights law firm, reminds purchasers of common stock of aTyr Pharma, Inc. (NASDAQ: ATYR) between January 16, 2025 and September 12, 2025, both dates inclusive (the "Class Period"), of the important December 8, 2025 lead plaintiff deadline.

So what: If you purchased aTyr Pharma common stock during the Class Period you may be entitled to compensation without payment of any out of pocket fees or costs through a contingency fee arrangement.

What to do next: To join the aTyr Pharma class action, go to https://rosenlegal.com/submit-form/?case_id=46109 or call Phillip Kim, Esq. toll-free at 866-767-3653 or email case@rosenlegal.com for information on the class action. A class action lawsuit has already been filed. If you wish to serve as lead plaintiff, you must move the Court no later than December 8, 2025. A lead plaintiff is a representative party acting on behalf of other class members in directing the litigation.

Why Rosen Law: We encourage investors to select qualified counsel with a track record of success in leadership roles. Often, firms issuing notices do not have comparable experience, resources, or any meaningful peer recognition. Many of these firms do not actually litigate securities class actions, but are merely middlemen that refer clients or partner with law firms that actually litigate the cases. Be wise in selecting counsel. The Rosen Law Firm represents investors throughout the globe, concentrating its practice in securities class actions and shareholder derivative litigation. Rosen Law Firm has achieved, at that time, the largest ever securities class action settlement against a Chinese Company. Rosen Law Firm was Ranked No. 1 by ISS Securities Class Action Services for number of securities class action settlements in 2017. The firm has been ranked in the top 4 each year since 2013 and has recovered hundreds of millions of dollars for investors. In 2019 alone the firm secured over $438 million for investors. In 2020, founding partner Laurence Rosen was named by law360 as a Titan of Plaintiffs' Bar. Many of the firm's attorneys have been recognized by Lawdragon and Super Lawyers.

Details of the case: According to the complaint, defendants provided overwhelmingly positive statements to investors while, at the same time, disseminating false and misleading statements and/or concealing material adverse facts concerning the efficacy of Efzofitimod, particularly, the drug's capability to allow a patient to completely taper their steroid usage.
When the true details entered the market, the lawsuit claims that investors suffered damages.

To join the aTyr Pharma class action, go to https://rosenlegal.com/submit-form/?case_id=46109 call Phillip Kim, Esq. toll-free at 866-767-3653 or email case@rosenlegal.com for information on the class action.

No Class Has Been Certified. Until a class is certified, you are not represented by counsel unless you retain one. You may select counsel of your choice. You may also remain an absent class member and do nothing at this point. An investor's ability to share in any potential future recovery is not dependent upon serving as lead plaintiff.

Follow us for updates on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/the-rosen-law-firm, on Twitter: https://twitter.com/rosen_firm or on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/rosenlawfirm/.

Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee a similar outcome.

Contact Information:

data.title: Disney's deal with YouTube TV shows how streamers are increasingly flexing their muscle
data.description:
By Lukas I. Alpert

An agreement to bring Disney programming back to YouTube TV subscribers after a two-week blackout shows how the center of gravity has shifted from linear television to streaming

YouTube has become a dominant force in the media landscape and has been using its position to negotiate tougher deals with its rivals - including Disney, with which it settled a two-week-long blackout on Friday.

With the eyeballs, goes the power.

The two-week-long dispute over carriage fees that left YouTube TV's 10 million subscribers unable to watch any Disney programming is now over, but its impact on the media landscape may be permanent.

The impasse was yet another signal of the shifting center of gravity in the media world - one moving away from linear-television stalwarts like the Walt Disney Co. (DIS), and toward emerging streaming giants like Alphabet Inc.'s (GOOGL) (GOOG) YouTube TV and others players.

The financial terms of the deal were not disclosed, but Disney said that the result would be a return of all of its programming to YouTube TV - including ESPN networks, ABC, Disney-branded channels, FX and National Geographic.

Additionally, YouTube TV subscribers will get access to ESPN's new premium streaming service at no additional cost.

"This new agreement reflects our continued commitment to delivering exceptional entertainment and evolving with how audiences choose to watch," Disney Entertainment co-Chairmen Alan Bergman and Dana Walden and ESPN Chairman Jimmy Pitaro said in a joint statement late Friday. "It recognizes the tremendous value of Disney's programming and provides YouTube TV subscribers with more flexibility and choice."

The lengthy programming blackout on YouTubeTV represented a modern twist on an old kind of fight. For years, channels would temporarily disappear from cable services amid disputes over carriage fees, or what carriers pay television companies for the rights to show their programming.

But the breakdown in negotiations between Disney and YouTube TV shows that streaming is now the prime battleground for control of a vital revenue stream. It's a conflict in which streamers appear far more willing to flex their muscle as their command of audiences continues to grow.

YouTube TV still sits behind major traditional cable operators like Comcast Corp.'s (CMCSA) Xfinity, Charter Communications Inc.'s (CHTR) Spectrum and TPG Inc.'s (TPG) DirecTV in terms of paying audience - but it is fast catching up. When its free, ad-driven YouTube platform is included, the company now draws the largest percentage of viewer hours of any company, Disney and Netflix Inc. (NFLX) included.

Some analysts predict that YouTube will surpass Disney as the largest media company, in terms of revenue, as early as this year.

The dispute was the latest in a string of disagreements between YouTube TV and major television companies. The streaming service, which is popular with viewers looking to watch live programming like sports and news, has had several other fraught carriage-fee fights with major media companies like Fox Corp. (FOXA) (FOX), Comcast's NBCUniversal and Paramount Skydance Corp. (PSKY) (Fox Corp. and MarketWatch parent News Corp share common ownership.) Each of those conflicts ended with last-minute agreements before any disruption to programming could occur.

A similar fight with TelevisaUnivision (TV) (MX:TLEVISACPO), however, reached an impasse in September, forcing the Spanish-language broadcaster's programming off YouTube TV's platform. That led President Donald Trump to press Google to make a deal and restore TelevisaUnivision's programming.

-Lukas I. Alpert

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  11-15-25 1222ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: New to The Street Airs Landmark Show #700 Today at 6:30 PM ET on Bloomberg Television
data.description:
Featured companies include Lionscrest Advisors, DataVault AI (NASDAQ:DVLT), Pudgy Penguins (PENGU)), Peer To Peer Network (OTC:PTOP), and Acurx Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:ACXP).

NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / November 15, 2025 / New to The Street, one of the nation's leading business television platforms and a top-ranked financial YouTube channel, announces the broadcast of its milestone Show #700 airing today at 6:30 PM ET on Bloomberg Television as sponsored programming.

This landmark episode underscores the brand's rapid expansion across national television, digital, social, and outdoor media - showcasing innovative public and private companies shaping the next decade of technology, finance, healthcare, and consumer culture.

Featured Companies on Episode #700

Lionscrest Advisors

A global advisory and investment firm known for its strategic insight across capital markets, M&A, and corporate finance. Lionscrest provides institutional-grade advisory services to clients navigating complex financial landscapes worldwide.

DataVault AI (NASDAQ:DVLT)

A leader in enterprise-grade artificial intelligence, digital asset monetization, and data management. DVLT continues to advance AI-driven business intelligence solutions that help organizations unlock and monetize their data ecosystems.

Pudgy Penguins (PudgyPenguins)

A global consumer brand that has successfully expanded from blockchain culture into toys, licensing, digital IP, and mainstream media. Pudgy Penguins is among the fastest-growing Web3-native franchises with international retail distribution.

Peer To Peer Network (OTC:PTOP)

The company behind MobiCard, a digital business card and networking system that is transforming how individuals and businesses connect, share, and track professional interactions through mobile and cloud-based technology.

Acurx Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:ACXP)

A late-stage biopharmaceutical company developing a new class of antibiotics targeting difficult-to-treat bacterial infections. ACXP's pipeline addresses major unmet medical needs, including C. difficile and other Gram-positive pathogens.

About New to The Street

New to The Street is the leading multi-channel financial media platform featuring weekly sponsored programming on Bloomberg Television and Fox Business, combined with one of the fastest-growing business YouTube channels in the industry. The platform integrates long-form interviews, television commercials, earned media, and iconic Times Square billboard exposure, offering companies a comprehensive and predictable national media presence.

With over 3.8M YouTube subscribers, national cable distribution reaching more than 200 million homes, and powerful social channels across LinkedIn, X, Instagram, and Facebook, New to The Street delivers unmatched visibility for emerging growth companies, mid-cap leaders, and global brands.

Media Contact; Monica Brennan Monica@NewtoTheStreet.com

data.title: Best Silver IRA Companies in the US (Top Silver IRA Rollover Companies Ranked)
data.description:
IRAEmpire has published its new and updated rankings of the best silver IRA providers in the USA this year. The rankings are aimed at educating consumers to help them make better-informed decisions.

DALLAS, TEXAS / ACCESS Newswire / November 15, 2025 / As inflation pressures, volatile markets, and geopolitical uncertainty continue to reshape retirement planning in the United States, more investors are looking beyond traditional assets like stocks and bonds. One option that has seen significant growth is the Silver IRA - a self-directed IRA that allows investors to hold physical silver coins and bullion inside a tax-advantaged retirement account.

However, selecting the right Silver IRA company is essential. Fees, storage security, IRS compliance, and customer support can vary dramatically between providers. To help investors make informed decisions, this guide evaluates and ranks the Best Silver IRA Companies of 2025, based on reputation, transparency, product offerings, and overall investor value.

Overview of Best Silver IRA:

  1. Augusta Precious Metals (Best for Large Investments)

  2. Lear Capital (Best Customer Support)

  3. Goldencrest Metals (Best Prices)

  4. Preserve Gold (Best Products)

After a comprehensive analysis, Augusta Precious Metals ranks #1, followed by Lear Capital, Goldencrest Metals, and Preserve Gold - each offering distinct advantages depending on your retirement goals and investment strategy.

In the sections ahead, you'll learn how a Silver IRA works, why silver is a compelling retirement asset, and how it compares to a Gold IRA - along with detailed, unbiased evaluations of the top companies.

Find The Best Precious Metals IRA Company of Your State Here.

What Is a Silver IRA?

A Silver IRA is a type of self-directed individual retirement account (IRA) that allows you to hold physical, IRS-approved silver coins and bullion as part of your retirement portfolio. Unlike a traditional IRA that holds paper assets such as stocks, bonds, and mutual funds, a Silver IRA gives investors the ability to own a tangible asset - one that historically retains value during inflation and economic uncertainty.

How a Silver IRA Works

A Silver IRA follows the same IRS rules as any traditional or Roth IRA, but with a few important distinctions:

  • Custodian Requirement:
    You must open the account through an IRS-approved self-directed IRA custodian. This custodian handles documentation, reporting, and compliance.

  • IRS-Approved Silver Only:
    Not all silver products qualify. Eligible items must meet specific purity standards, typically 99.9% pure silver (0.999). Examples include:

    • American Silver Eagles

    • Canadian Silver Maple Leafs

    • Silver bars from approved refiners

  • Mandatory Depository Storage:
    Physical silver held in an IRA must be stored in an IRS-approved depository, such as Delaware Depository, Brinks, or IDS of Texas.
    Home storage is not allowed and would be considered a taxable distribution.

  • Tax Benefits:
    Like any IRA, Silver IRAs can be either:

    • Traditional - tax-deferred growth and potentially tax-deductible contributions.

    • Roth - tax-free withdrawals in retirement (if qualifications are met).

Funding a Silver IRA

You can fund a Silver IRA through:

  • New contributions

  • IRA-to-IRA transfer

  • 401(k) to IRA rollover (if the plan is eligible)

These methods allow you to move existing retirement savings into a Silver IRA without triggering taxes or penalties when done correctly.

Why Investors Choose a Silver IRA

Silver's combination of affordability, industrial demand, and long-term value preservation makes it an attractive option for investors looking to diversify beyond traditional financial markets. The ability to hold physical metal also appeals to those who prefer assets not tied to corporate or governmental performance.

Augusta Precious Metals - Ranked #1 and Best Overall for Transparency and Education

Founded in 2012, Augusta Precious Metals has become one of the most trusted names in the U.S. precious metals industry. While widely known for its Gold IRA services, Augusta also provides a strong selection of IRS-approved silver products and places heavy emphasis on investor education, transparency, and long-term support. These qualities make Augusta the clear #1 Silver IRA company for 2025.

Read Augusta Precious Metals Reviews, Feedback and More

Reputation and Trustworthiness

Augusta holds:

  • A+ rating with the BBB

  • AAA rating with the BCA

  • Strong customer satisfaction across independent platforms

What sets Augusta apart is its consistent focus on clarity - especially important for retirees who want to understand risk, volatility, and long-term returns before investing in silver.

Silver IRA Product Selection

Augusta offers a curated catalog of IRA-eligible silver, including:

  • American Silver Eagles

  • Canadian Silver Maple Leafs

  • Silver rounds and bars from recognized refiners

  • Other IRS-approved silver bullion products

All items meet purity requirements of .999 fine silver, making them eligible for self-directed IRAs.

Fees and Transparency

Augusta is known for clear, upfront disclosure.
They provide detailed explanations of:

  • Setup costs

  • Annual custodian and storage fees

  • Spreads and product pricing

There are no hidden charges, making Augusta one of the most transparent Silver IRA providers in the U.S. market.

Storage and Security

Silver purchased through Augusta is stored in IRS-approved depositories, such as:

  • Delaware Depository

  • Brinks Global Services

These facilities offer robust security, insurance coverage, and clean audit records - essential for protecting physical silver, which requires more vault space than gold.

Education and Customer Support

Augusta stands out for its no-pressure, education-first model.
The company offers:

  • One-on-one web sessions

  • Detailed IRA guides

  • Market insights

  • Support throughout rollovers and transfers

Retirement investors who value clarity, patience, and professional guidance consistently rate Augusta as the top choice.

Ideal For

Augusta Precious Metals is best suited for:

  • Investors who want maximum transparency and trust

  • Retirees seeking long-term wealth protection

  • First-time precious metals investors who need clear, educational support

  • Those who prefer a highly reputable firm over aggressive sales-driven companies

In summary, Augusta Precious Metals ranks #1 for Silver IRAs due to its exceptional transparency, strong educational focus, and high trust ratings - making it the best overall choice for U.S. investors entering the silver market.

Learn More About Augusta Precious Metals Here.

Lear Capital - Ranked #2 Among the Best Silver IRA Companies for Customer Service & Product Variety

Founded in 1997, Lear Capital is one of the most established and recognizable precious metals companies in the United States. With nearly three decades of experience and a strong national presence, Lear offers a wide selection of IRA-eligible silver products, competitive pricing, and a reputation for responsive customer support. These strengths make Lear Capital the #2 Silver IRA company in 2025.

Read Lear Capital Reviews, Feedback and More

Reputation and Credibility

Lear Capital maintains:

  • A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau (BBB)

  • Strong reviews across Trustpilot and Consumer Affairs

  • A long history of serving U.S. retirement investors

Its longevity and brand recognition make it a trusted choice for investors who want a well-established firm with a proven track record.

Silver IRA Product Range

Lear Capital offers one of the largest selections of IRA-eligible silver, including:

  • American Silver Eagles

  • Silver Canadian Maple Leafs

  • Silver bars from accredited refiners

  • IRA-approved rounds and specialty bullion

This broad selection allows investors to build a diversified silver portfolio tailored to their risk tolerance and long-term goals.

Fees and Pricing Transparency

Lear provides clear, upfront information about:

  • IRA setup fees

  • Annual maintenance and storage costs

  • Pricing structures for silver coins and bars

While fees may vary depending on custodian or depository, Lear's representatives make cost breakdowns easy to understand, which is especially important for new Silver IRA investors.

Customer Service and Support

One of Lear Capital's biggest advantages is its customer service quality. The company is known for:

  • Fast response times

  • Knowledgeable account representatives

  • Step-by-step assistance through rollovers and transfers

  • Educational materials covering silver market dynamics

This level of support makes the process smoother for retirees or first-time precious metals investors.

Storage and Security

Lear partners with several IRS-approved depositories, including:

  • Brinks Global Services

  • Delaware Depository

  • Other secure vaulting providers across the U.S.

These facilities offer insured, audited storage with strong compliance standards-especially important for silver, which requires more vault space than gold.

Ideal For

Lear Capital is best suited for:

  • Investors who want a wide range of IRA-eligible silver products

  • Individuals who value responsive customer service

  • New and experienced investors needing clear rollover guidance

  • Those who prefer working with a long-established national brand

Lear Capital ranks #2 for Silver IRAs because of its strong customer support, extensive product offerings, and decades of industry experience-making it a trusted partner for U.S. retirement investors.

Learn More About Lear Capital's Precious Metals IRA

Goldencrest Metals - Ranked #3 Among the Best Silver Companies for Competitive Pricing

Founded in 2020, Goldencrest Metals is a fast-growing precious metals provider known for its affordable pricing, transparent fee structure, and personalized service. While newer than some of its competitors, Goldencrest has quickly earned a reputation in the Silver IRA market for offering some of the most competitive markups and a smooth, beginner-friendly onboarding process. These strengths make it the #3 Silver IRA company for 2025.

Read Goldencrest Metals Reviews, Feedback and More

Reputation and Growth

Despite being a relatively young company, Goldencrest has built a strong reputation through:

  • Positive reviews on independent consumer platforms

  • A no-pressure, education-first approach

  • Clear communication throughout the IRA setup and rollover process

Investors consistently highlight the company's transparency and professionalism - qualities that are especially valuable when dealing with physical silver, which has higher volume and storage considerations than gold.

Low Markups & Transparent Fees

Goldencrest stands out for offering some of the lowest silver markups in the industry.
Their pricing advantages include:

  • Competitive premiums on silver coins and bars

  • No hidden administrative or "surprise" charges

  • Simple, upfront explanations of all IRA-related fees

For investors who want to maximize the number of silver ounces they can add to their IRA, Goldencrest's cost-efficiency is a major advantage.

Silver IRA Product Selection

The company offers a solid selection of IRS-approved silver products, including:

  • American Silver Eagles

  • Silver Maple Leafs

  • Silver bars from approved refiners

  • IRA-eligible rounds and bullion products

All products meet the IRS requirement of .999 (99.9%) purity, ensuring full compliance with Silver IRA regulations.

Storage and Security

Goldencrest Metals partners with trusted, IRS-approved vaulting facilities such as:

  • Brinks Global Services

  • IDS (International Depository Services)

These depositories offer secure, insured storage with regular audits - crucial for silver holdings, which require more storage space per dollar invested compared to gold.

Customer Service and Personal Attention

Because Goldencrest is a growing company with a streamlined operation, many investors appreciate its highly personalized service. Account specialists provide:

  • One-on-one consultations

  • Assistance with IRA rollovers or transfers

  • Guidance on selecting IRA-eligible silver products

  • Clear explanations tailored to first-time investors

This personal-touch approach differentiates Goldencrest from larger, more corporate providers.

Ideal For

Goldencrest Metals is best suited for:

  • Cost-conscious investors looking for affordable silver pricing

  • New Silver IRA investors who want clear, step-by-step support

  • Individuals looking for personalized service rather than large-company bureaucracy

  • Investors seeking a low minimum investment compared to premium-tier providers

Goldencrest Metals ranks #3 for Silver IRAs due to its competitive pricing, transparency, and hands-on service - making it especially attractive for investors who want to maximize silver accumulation without excessive costs.

Learn More About Goldencrest Metals' Precious Metals IRA

Preserve Gold - Ranked #4 Among the Best Silver IRA Companies for Diversification Across Metals

Founded in 2021, Preserve Gold is a trusted precious metals provider offering a broad selection of physical assets, including silver, gold, platinum, and palladium. Its strength lies in its ability to help investors build multi-metal IRA portfolios, making it an appealing choice for those who want diversification beyond just silver. With clear pricing, accessible customer support, and a simplified onboarding process, Preserve Gold ranks as the #4 Silver IRA company for 2025.

Read Preserve Gold Reviews, Feedback and More

Reputation and Trust

Although newer than many competitors, Preserve Gold has earned strong ratings on platforms like:

  • BBB

  • Trustpilot

  • Consumer review sites

Investors frequently note the company's helpful representatives and transparent explanations of IRA rules, fees, and rollover requirements. This is especially valuable for newcomers who may feel overwhelmed by the complexities of self-directed IRAs.

Silver IRA Product Selection

Preserve Gold offers a solid lineup of IRS-approved silver products such as:

  • American Silver Eagles

  • Silver Maple Leafs

  • Silver bars and rounds from accredited refiners

  • IRA-eligible bullion options

All silver products meet the .999 purity standard required by the IRS for inclusion in a Silver IRA.

Strength in Diversification

Preserve Gold's key advantage is its ability to provide a wide variety of precious metals:

  • Silver for industrial-driven growth

  • Gold for long-term wealth protection

  • Platinum & Palladium for advanced diversification strategies

This makes Preserve Gold ideal for investors who want flexibility in building a multi-metal IRA tailored to different market conditions.

Fee Structure and Transparency

The company maintains a straightforward fee model that clearly outlines:

  • Setup and administrative fees

  • Annual storage fees

  • Pricing spreads on silver and other metals

There are no hidden charges or complicated pricing tiers - helping investors make confident, informed decisions.

Storage and Security

Preserve Gold partners with reputable, IRS-approved depositories, including:

  • Delaware Depository

  • Brinks Global Services

These secure vaults provide insurance, regularly audited inventory systems, and high-level protection needed for storing large volumes of silver.

Customer Support & Education

Preserve Gold offers strong customer assistance with:

  • Step-by-step IRA setup

  • Guidance on metal selection

  • Help with 401(k) and IRA rollovers

  • Educational materials explaining the fundamentals of Silver IRAs

The focus is on clarity and empowerment, not aggressive sales tactics.

Ideal For

Preserve Gold is best suited for:

  • Investors who want diversification across gold, silver, platinum, and palladium

  • New investors who prefer clear, simple onboarding

  • Individuals building a portfolio that balances stability and growth potential

  • Those who want a flexible provider with a wide metal selection

Preserve Gold ranks #4 among the best Silver IRA companies for its diversified precious metal offerings, transparent pricing, and easy onboarding - making it a strong choice for investors looking to build a balanced, multi-metal IRA.

Learn About Preserve Gold's Precious Metals IRA Here.

Why a Silver IRA Is a Strong Choice for Retirement Investors

While gold often receives the most attention in the precious metals market, silver plays a uniquely valuable role in long-term retirement planning. A Silver IRA provides access to an asset that combines monetary value, industrial utility, and long-term growth potential - making it an appealing option for investors who want diversification beyond traditional IRAs.

1. Lower Entry Cost Compared to Gold

One of silver's biggest advantages is price accessibility.
Because silver is significantly cheaper per ounce than gold, investors can accumulate more ounces for the same investment amount.

This benefits retirement savers who:

  • Want to start with smaller contributions

  • Prefer steady accumulation over time

  • Are building a diversified metals portfolio on a budget

The low barrier to entry makes Silver IRAs especially attractive for newer investors or those who want to hedge inflation without large upfront costs.

2. Strong Industrial Demand and Growth Potential

Silver has a dual identity - part precious metal, part industrial metal.
It is used extensively in:

  • Solar panels

  • Electric vehicles and EV batteries

  • 5G infrastructure

  • Medical devices

  • Semiconductor production

  • Aerospace and defense technologies

As the U.S. and global economies shift toward electrification and renewable energy, industrial demand for silver is projected to rise. This demand can potentially increase long-term value and create stronger upside momentum during periods of economic expansion.

3. Historical Performance During Inflation

Like gold, silver has historically acted as a hedge against inflation.
When the dollar weakens or purchasing power drops, tangible assets such as silver tend to hold their value.

During high-inflation periods, silver has often seen sharper surges than gold due to its smaller market size and industrial sensitivity, giving investors potential growth along with inflation protection.

4. Portfolio Diversification and Risk Reduction

Silver behaves differently than traditional paper assets. Adding silver to a retirement account:

  • Reduces dependence on the stock market

  • Offers protection during market downturns

  • Adds a hedge against systemic financial risks

  • Balances volatility within a broader asset mix

For many U.S. investors, a Silver IRA becomes a complementary component of a diversified retirement strategy - one that can help stabilize long-term returns.

5. Tangible, Physical Ownership

A Silver IRA holds real, physical metal, not digital or paper-based silver instruments.
This means investors own something:

  • That cannot be created or inflated by governments

  • That is backed by intrinsic value

  • That has survived centuries of economic cycles

For retirement savers who distrust overexposed markets or excessive money printing, physical silver offers a sense of security and independence.

Silver IRA vs Gold IRA: Key Differences

Both Silver IRAs and Gold IRAs allow investors to hold physical precious metals within a tax-advantaged retirement account. While they share similar IRS rules and structural features, the two assets behave differently in the market, making each suitable for different types of investors and financial goals. Understanding these differences helps retirees and pre-retirees decide which metal - or combination of metals - best fits their long-term strategy.

1. Price and Accessibility

Silver is drastically more affordable than gold, making it easier for investors with modest budgets to enter the precious metals market.

  • Silver: Lower per-ounce cost → better for dollar-cost averaging or incremental purchases.

  • Gold: Higher per-ounce cost → preferred by high-net-worth investors seeking concentrated value.

Because silver is cheaper, investors can accumulate more physical ounces for the same investment amount, which some consider an advantage when preparing for long-term uncertainty.

2. Volatility and Market Movement

Silver tends to be more volatile than gold due to its dual role as both a precious and industrial metal.

  • When industrial demand surges, silver prices can rise faster than gold.

  • During economic slowdowns, silver can decline more sharply because of reduced industrial usage.

  • Gold is historically more stable, making it a steadier hedge during crises.

Bottom line:
Silver = higher upside potential but also higher short-term volatility.
Gold = stability, long-term preservation of value.

3. Industrial Utility

Gold has limited industrial usage, while silver is essential to multiple global industries:

  • Solar energy and photovoltaics

  • Semiconductors and electronics manufacturing

  • Electric vehicle systems

  • Medical devices

  • 5G communications

  • Defense and aerospace

This industrial demand gives silver unique growth drivers that gold does not have. As the U.S. shifts toward renewable energy and electrification, silver demand may continue to strengthen.

4. Storage and Volume Considerations

Silver requires more storage space due to its lower value per ounce.

Example:

  • $50,000 in gold may fit in a small vault compartment.

  • $50,000 in silver could take up several times the space due to its volume.

This can slightly increase storage costs for large silver holdings, depending on the custodian and depository.

5. Hedging Strategy and Portfolio Role

Silver and gold serve different functions in a retirement portfolio:

  • Gold → Wealth Preservation
    Stable, historically reliable, ideal for long-term security.

  • Silver → Growth and Diversification
    Offers more upside potential and greater exposure to industrial trends.

  • Combined Strategy
    Many U.S. investors choose a mix of gold and silver in their IRA to balance both stability and growth.

6. Liquidity and Buyback Differences

Both metals are liquid, but gold tends to be easier to sell quickly at institutional levels.
Silver remains highly liquid but may have larger premiums depending on the product type and market conditions.

How IRAEmpire Ranked the Best Silver IRA Companies

Choosing the right Silver IRA provider requires more than comparing prices or promotional claims. Retirement investors need companies that offer transparency, compliance, secure storage, and long-term support. To ensure this ranking remains balanced and fact-driven, each company was evaluated using the following measurable criteria.

Reputation, Ratings & Track Record

We reviewed each company's standing with trusted consumer protection organizations, including:

  • Better Business Bureau (BBB) ratings

  • Business Consumer Alliance (BCA) ratings

  • Trustpilot and Google Reviews

  • Years in business and consistency of customer satisfaction

Companies with longstanding reputations for honesty, professionalism, and ethical behavior scored higher.

Fee Transparency and Cost Structure

Silver IRAs often include several fees:

  • Account setup

  • Annual administration

  • Storage and insurance

  • Dealer markups

We prioritized companies that disclose all fees upfront, offer reasonable pricing, and avoid hidden charges. Firms with flat-rate fee structures or clearly explained percentage-based fees received higher rankings.

Product Selection and IRA-Eligible Silver

Not all silver products qualify for IRA use.
We reviewed each company's:

  • Availability of IRS-approved silver coins and bars

  • Access to reputable refiners and sovereign mints

  • Range of silver investment options (bars, rounds, coins)

Companies offering diverse, compliant, and clearly labeled IRA-eligible silver scored higher.

Storage Security and Custodian Partnerships

An IRS-approved Silver IRA requires secure, third-party storage.
We evaluated:

  • Depository partners (e.g., Brinks, Delaware Depository, IDS of Texas)

  • Segregated vs non-segregated storage options

  • Insurance coverage

  • Auditing frequency and security standards

Companies with proven, reputable storage partners ranked more favorably.

Buyback Policies and Liquidity

Retirees eventually need the ability to liquidate their metals easily.
Firms offering:

  • Transparent buyback programs

  • Competitive repurchase pricing

  • Fast processing times

received higher scores for investor convenience and long-term portfolio flexibility.

Customer Education and Support

Silver IRAs can be complex, especially for new investors.
We rated companies based on:

  • Educational materials (guides, webinars, articles)

  • Personal consultation availability

  • Responsiveness of customer support

  • Clarity during rollovers and transfers

Companies emphasizing client education-rather than high-pressure sales-ranked the highest.

Overall Value to the Investor

Finally, we evaluated the complete investor experience, including:

  • Transparency

  • Support quality

  • Long-term reliability

  • Ease of account setup

  • Compliance and reporting accuracy

Find the Best Silver IRA Provider of Your State

What to Consider Before Opening a Silver IRA

A Silver IRA can be a powerful way to diversify your retirement savings, but understanding the key factors involved is essential before committing. Silver behaves differently from gold and requires unique considerations related to storage, volatility, and long-term strategy. Below are the most important points investors should evaluate before opening a Silver IRA.

1. Minimum Investment Requirements

Each Silver IRA company sets its own minimum investment amount.
Typical minimums range from:

  • $10,000 to $50,000 for most providers

  • Lower minimums from emerging companies like Goldencrest

  • Higher minimums from premium-tier providers like Augusta

Consider your investment budget and long-term allocation before choosing a company.

2. Fees and Storage Costs

Silver IRAs involve several ongoing fees:

  • Account setup fees

  • Annual custodian fees

  • Storage fees for the physical silver

  • Shipping or handling charges (depending on provider policies)

  • Dealer markups or spreads on silver products

Because silver has a lower value per ounce, it takes up more storage space than gold, which can influence storage costs over time.

Always ask for a complete fee schedule before opening an account.

3. IRS-Approved Silver Products Only

Not all forms of silver qualify for IRA use.
The IRS requires:

  • 99.9% pure silver (.999 fine)

  • Products from approved mints or refiners

  • No collectible or numismatic silver coins

Choose companies that offer clear labeling and guidance on IRA-eligible products.

4. Market Volatility and Industrial Demand

Silver prices fluctuate more than gold because of industrial demand in:

  • Electronics

  • Solar technology

  • EV batteries

  • Medical and defense applications

This dual role creates higher upside potential but can also lead to sharper price swings.
Investors should be prepared for moderate volatility and maintain a long-term perspective.

5. Storage Requirements

Physical silver cannot be stored at home in an IRA.
The IRS requires:

  • Storage in an approved depository

  • Custodian oversight

  • Segregated or non-segregated vault options

Depositories such as Brinks, Delaware Depository, and IDS are commonly used for Silver IRAs.

6. Liquidity and Buyback Policies

Eventually, retirees may want to:

  • Liquidate their silver

  • Transfer metals

  • Take an in-kind distribution

The best companies offer:

  • Transparent buyback programs

  • Competitive pricing

  • Fast and simple liquidation processes

A solid buyback policy can greatly simplify retirement withdrawals.

7. Rollover and Transfer Rules

A Silver IRA can be funded through:

  • 401(k) to IRA rollovers

  • IRA-to-IRA transfers

  • Annual contributions (within IRS limits)

When done correctly via a trustee-to-trustee transfer, investors can avoid taxes or early withdrawal penalties.

Choose a company that provides clear rollover guidance to maintain compliance.

8. Long-Term Portfolio Strategy

Silver should complement - not replace - other retirement assets.
Investors should consider:

  • What percentage of their IRA should be allocated to precious metals

  • Whether they want silver only or a mix of silver and gold

  • How long they plan to hold the assets

  • Their risk tolerance and retirement timeline

A balanced, diversified approach typically provides the most stability.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

1. Are Silver IRAs tax-deductible?

A Silver IRA follows the same tax rules as a traditional or Roth IRA.

  • Traditional Silver IRA:
    Contributions may be tax-deductible, and growth is tax-deferred until retirement withdrawals.

  • Roth Silver IRA:
    Contributions are after-tax, but qualified withdrawals in retirement are tax-free.

Your eligibility for deductions depends on income, filing status, and participation in employer retirement plans.

2. Is silver better than gold for retirement investing?

It depends on your goals.

  • Silver offers more upside potential due to strong industrial demand but is more volatile.

  • Gold is better for long-term stability and wealth preservation.

Many investors choose both, using silver for growth and gold for security.

3. Can I store my Silver IRA coins at home?

No.
Silver held inside an IRA must be stored in an IRS-approved depository under the custody of a qualified IRA custodian.

Storing IRA silver at home is considered a taxable distribution, which may also incur a 10% penalty if you're under age 59½.

4. What types of silver are eligible for a Silver IRA?

IRS-approved products include:

  • American Silver Eagle coins

  • Canadian Silver Maple Leaf coins

  • Australian Silver coins (various series)

  • Silver bars and rounds from accredited refiners

  • All products must be 99.9% pure silver (.999 fine)

Collectible or numismatic coins are not eligible.

5. How do I fund a Silver IRA?

You can fund a Silver IRA through:

  • A direct IRA-to-IRA transfer

  • A 401(k) or 403(b) rollover (if eligible)

  • New annual contributions (within IRS limits)

Trustee-to-trustee transfers help avoid taxes and early withdrawal penalties.

6. Is silver a good hedge against inflation?

Yes.
Silver has historically increased in value during periods of:

  • High inflation

  • Currency devaluation

  • Market uncertainty

  • Rising industrial demand

While more volatile than gold, silver has shown strong performance during inflationary cycles.

7. How liquid is a Silver IRA?

A Silver IRA is highly liquid, provided the company offers:

  • A transparent buyback program

  • Competitive market-based pricing

  • Fast processing times

Top providers like Augusta, Lear, Goldencrest, and Preserve Gold simplify the liquidation process for retirement distributions.

8. Does a Silver IRA require more storage space than a Gold IRA?

Yes.
Silver is much cheaper per ounce, so the same dollar investment results in more ounces and greater vault space.
This can affect storage fees over time, depending on your custodian and depository.

Summary of IRAEmpire's Findings:

Silver IRAs have become an increasingly attractive option for Americans who want to diversify their retirement savings, hedge against inflation, and benefit from silver's growing industrial demand. Unlike gold, which is primarily valued for its role as a safe-haven asset, silver offers a blend of monetary protection and industrial utility, creating compelling long-term growth potential.

  • #1 Augusta Precious Metals - Best overall for transparency, education, and long-term support

  • #2 Lear Capital - Best for product variety and reliable customer service

  • #3 Goldencrest Metals - Best for competitive pricing and beginner-friendly guidance

  • #4 Preserve Gold - Best for multi-metal diversification and simple onboarding

Each provider caters to different investor needs, whether you prioritize low fees, educational resources, personalized support, or access to a wide range of metals.

About IRAEmpire

IRAEmpire is a leading online resource dedicated to helping investors make smarter decisions about retirement planning, precious metals, and alternative investments. Built with a mission to simplify complex financial topics, IRAEmpire provides in-depth reviews, comparison guides, and educational content focused on Gold IRAs, Silver IRAs, and other self-directed retirement accounts.

Key features include detailed breakdowns of top Gold IRA companies, step-by-step investment guides, and market trend analysis. By combining data-driven research with easy-to-understand explanations, IRAEmpire empowers readers to compare providers, evaluate fees, and choose the right partner for their long-term financial security.

Whether you're a first-time investor exploring precious metals or an experienced retiree looking to diversify, IRAEmpire serves as a trusted guide. Its goal is simple: to help you protect and grow your wealth through smart, informed retirement planning.

ryan@iraempire.com
